question_title: Will the White House or OMB publish FY 2027 President’s Budget materials before April 30, 2026?
summary: Multiple credible sources (Roll Call, Bloomberg Government, NLIHC, E&E News) report the White House is targeting the week of March 30, 2026 for release of FY 2027 budget materials — just 5 days from now. Bloomberg Government specifically cites March 31 as the target date. The Pentagon comptroller confirmed on March 17 that release is "very close." The question's resolution criteria are broad: any FY 2027 budget materials on the OMB page qualify, including partial/skinny budgets. Even if the March 30 target slips, there are 35 days of buffer before the April 30 deadline. The only path to NO requires a 5+ week delay beyond the current target — an unprecedented scenario given the political pressure from House appropriators planning mid-April markups. The FY 2026 skinny budget (first year of admin) was released May 2, 2025 — just barely after what would be an April 30 cutoff. FY 2027 is expected earlier. Minor risks include the DHS shutdown standoff and Iran war supplemental consuming OMB bandwidth, but no source reports these as delaying the budget timeline.

factors.description: Multiple credible sources (Roll Call, Bloomberg Government, NLIHC, E&E News) report White House targeting week of March 30 for FY 2027 budget release, with Bloomberg specifically citing March 31
Pentagon comptroller said March 17 that release is 'very close' — weeks away, with J-books in April
Question allows 'any official FY 2027 President's Budget materials' — even a skinny/partial budget qualifies, lowering the bar significantly
35 days remain until April 30 deadline — even if March 30 target slips by weeks, there is substantial buffer
FY 2026 skinny budget was released May 2, 2025 (first year of admin) — FY 2027 is expected earlier
DHS shutdown standoff and Iran war supplemental could distract OMB attention and potentially delay budget work
Trump has never released a full budget proposal since taking office — could indicate willingness to delay further
House appropriators planning markups in mid-April create strong pressure for budget release before then

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.96 (96% YES)**

The evidence strongly supports resolution YES. The White House is targeting the week of March 30 for FY 2027 budget release, per multiple independent credible sources. With 35 days of buffer before the April 30 deadline and a low bar for resolution (any official FY 2027 materials qualify), the question would only resolve NO if an unprecedented delay of 5+ weeks occurred beyond the current target — a scenario unsupported by any reporting.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the White House or OMB publish FY 2027 President’s Budget materials before April 30, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42808)
[CRFB Statement on Overdue President’s FY 2027 Budget | Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget](https://www.crfb.org/press-releases/crfb-statement-overdue-presidents-fy-2027-budget)
[White House targets week of March 30 for budget release](https://rollcall.com/2026/03/04/white-house-targets-week-of-march-30-for-budget-release/)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if FY 2027 President’s Budget materials are publicly posted before April 30, 2026 on the official OMB President’s Budget page at https://www.whitehouse.gov/omb/information-resources/budget/. It resolves to no otherwise.
fine_print: Any official FY 2027 President’s Budget materials posted on that page qualify, including the main budget release page and linked budget documents. Use the first public posting date shown by the White House or OMB. Candidate Sources: White House Briefing Room https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefing-room/
